key_learnings: Total daily water needs are highly variable (1.5–3.5L/day) and depend on body size, activity level, climate, and dietary water content. Mild dehydration (1–2% body weight loss) impairs cognitive performance, mood, and physical endurance. Water-rich foods (fruits, vegetables, soups) contribute 20–30% of total daily fluid intake.
research_findings: Pre-meal water consumption (500 mL 30 min before eating) reduces caloric intake by ~13% and aids weight management. Urine color (pale yellow = well-hydrated) is a more practical and reliable hydration indicator than thirst alone. Electrolyte replacement (sodium, potassium, magnesium) is essential during prolonged exercise or heat exposure to prevent hyponatremia.
current_focus_areas: Developing personalized daily hydration targets based on activity and environment; building electrolyte balance guidance for athletic populations; researching the metabolic effects of chronic mild dehydration on kidney function and energy metabolism.
